MINI MINUTES:

Off Calendar Mar-29-2023 Continued To Jun-07-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances; Jeralyn Spradlin appearing via zoom for and with Alicia Lui appearing via zoom.

The Court noted that a Petition for final distribution including a copy of the family trust is past due. Mrs. Spradlin stated that she filed it back in 2019 and has filed amendments since then, and that the fourth amendment response to all the Courts inquires and that she got signatures from all the beneficiaries except the guardian ad litem ("GAL"). She also stated there is a finalized version of the family trust that was executed by everybody except the (GAL) and that there is a request for waiver in the fourth amended petition. In addition, Ms. Spradlin stated that the only asset from this estate is going into the family trust to protect the daughter who is handicapped and that is a minor. The Court explained that the second amended petition was taken off calendar by the Court and that she was required to attach a copy of the executed trust to a petition for final distribution. The Court further explained that she would need to file a new petition for final distribution with the executed trust attached to it. Status on close of estate is continued to June 07, 2023, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition is to be filed by June 02, 2023.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Maloney. Not reported.

Petition For Court Approval Of Settlement Agreement Of Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Sep-29-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeralyn Spradlin, via Zoom, for and with, Alicia Lui, Personal Representative.

The court acknowledges the proposed order in the e-filing queue, but points to the fact that a copy of the agreement is not attached and therefore it is incomplete. The court advises that it will not approve an open-ended agreement and that it can revise paragraph 17 of the agreement regarding CCP 664.6 or, it can take this matter under submission to allow counsel ten (10) days to submit a proposed order with a copy of the agreement attached via email to the assigned Examiner. Counsel confirms that she intends to send an updated proposed order with a copy of the agreement attached via email to the assigned Examiner. The court advises that if the preceding is not completed within ten days (10) the court will need to continue this matter.

The Petition for Court Approval of Settlement Agreement, etc., is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Aug-30-2021 Continued To Nov-01-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-18-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeralyn Spradlin appeared by zoom for and with Alicia Lui (Petitioner) by zoom.

The Court noted that the parties haven't made any progress with this matter. Discussion was held regarding the manner in which disclaimers are often used and the many consequences it causes for the parties, and people often asking to rescind their disclaimers once they have a full understanding of what it means. The Court noted that the Minor (David Lee's Son) is now the beneficiary, but the document does not list the Minor, that it lists the Father, and that the settlement agreement as drafted provides that money go into a trust, but there is no trust document. The Court also informed Ms. Spradlin that it is not prepared to take on multiple years of supervision. Ms. Spradlin stated that if the Court was going to approve the settlement, then they would draft a trust. The Court stated that it will not order distribution to a trust that is not in existence. Ms. Lui asked if it's too late to rescind the disclaimer for David Lee. The Court explained that they will need to file petition. The Amended Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement is continued to October 13, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to discuss the matter and determine whether they are going to fix the defects in the petition, or file a request to have the disclaimer rescinded.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Court Approval Of Settlement Agreement Of Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Continued To Nov-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeralyn Spradlin, Esq., counsel for Alicia Lui, Executor (appearing by video conference, audio only).

The Court advises Ms. Spradlin of certain concerns, including the David Lee disclaimer which, contrary to his ostensible understanding of the same, cedes his interest in the estate to his children rather than to Mr. Lee's siblings. Consequently, the approval of the settlement agreement will require the appointment of a guardian ad litem for Mr. Lee's children to effect independent review on their behalf. Ms. Spradlin responds, noting her understanding from prior counsel that the disclaimer had not been "accepted" by the Court and was, therefore, void. The Court notes no formal process for "accepting" a disclaimer, that it need only be filed whereupon it would become effective.

The Court inquires as to whether the trust proposed by the settlement agreement has been set up. Ms. Spradlin advises that they did not intend to set up the trust until the settlement agreement was approved by the Court. The Court advises that a copy of the trust instrument will need to be submitted to the Court for review before the Settlement Agreement can be approved.

The Court continues this matter to November 2, 2020 for appointment of a guardian ad litem, amendment to the settlement agreement as necessary, and submission of the trust instrument to the Court for review.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Court Examiner: Helen Yune Trowbridge; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. Bailiff: Deputy K. Kabasares. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-21-2019 Continued To Dec-02-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeralyn Spradlin appeared by telephone for Alicia Lui.

Ms. Spradlin stated that she didn't get the Probate Examiner's notes until Saturday. The Court stated that it got confirmation that the notes went through and that Ms. Spradlin needs to make sure the Court has accurate contact information. Ms. Spradlin stated that David's disclaimer was improper, that David is now claiming and that it is correct that there are no Minors that are parties to the settlement. The Court informed Counsel that she has to get the trust established, drafted and funded, and get the settlement agreement approved by the Court and then it can hear this petition. Ms. Spradlin stated that the estate only owns half of the real property and that the Brothers that own the other half have agreed to put their half in the trust. The Second Amended First and Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to December 2, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Spradlin is to file a petition for approval of the settlement agreement.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jul-11-2018 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alicia Lui in Propria Persona (Petitoner)

Ms. Lui stated that she is the daughter of the decedent and seeking appointment as administrator of her mother's estate to transfer her mother's 50% ownership of the family home to herself and her siblings. She also stated that the other 50% is owned by her two brothers; her disabled sister is living in the house, and they do not intend to sell the house. The Court advised the Petitioner of the responsibilities as Administrator, spoke of the complexity of the court rules that govern Probate and suggested she seek legal advice as these processes can be complicated. The Petitioner for Letters of Administration is Taken Under Submission. The Court informs Ms. Lui that if this petition is granted the following hearing dates will apply: Status on filing of Inventory and Appraisal will be set for July 11, 2018; Status on Close of Estate will be set for March 27, 2019; Bond will be waived.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
